SLA Metric: Defect Leakage: ~4.8%; Defect Density: 22.19 bugs/KLOC vs target 2 bugs/KLOC; 116 QA defects
Risk Title: Critical Quality Degradation with Systemic Defect Density and Leakage Risk
Risk Description: Because defect density of 22.19 bugs per KLOC exceeds the target of 2 bugs per KLOC by over 11 times (documented in Finance Metrics, MBR, and DSR), combined with 116 QA defects across sprints 15-20 and a defect leakage rate of 4.8% approaching the critical 5% threshold (documented in JIRA and WSR), and QA cost overruns of INR 0.28L (6.7%) driven by repeated regression and retesting cycles (documented in Finance Report), there is a risk that systemic code quality issues will result in escaped defects reaching UAT and production environments, which could lead to customer-reported production defects, extensive rework consuming development capacity, SLA breaches, increased support and maintenance costs, reputational damage, and potential contract penalties.
Tech Stack: QA Automation, JIRA, Testing Frameworks, .NET 8
Potential Impact: Production defects impacting end users; customer dissatisfaction and trust erosion; extensive rework and remediation costs; SLA breaches triggering contract penalties; increased support costs; reputational damage in finance sector; technical debt accumulation
